item.title: Create docs/PROBLEMS.md — the pain points aishore solves, with evidence
item.intent: People search for problems, not solutions — this document must rank for the frustrations that drive users to aishore.
item.description: This is the weakest content area. The only existing source is README.md's 'Why aishore?' section (two paragraphs: vibe coding breaks at scale, no memory between sessions, no quality gate, no batch work). The document needs to articulate specific, googleable failure modes: context loss between AI coding sessions, no regression detection in AI-generated code, no audit trail for AI development decisions, one-shot prompting fails for multi-feature work, no quality gate between AI implementation and merge, AI agents don't self-critique without structure. For each problem, describe the pain as the user experiences it, link evidence where possible (forum posts, tweets, articles about vibe coding limitations), and explain concretely how aishore addresses it. This is part documentation, part SEO, part positioning.
steps: Read README.md 'Why aishore?' section for the seed content (vibe coding limitations, no memory, no quality gate, no batch work)
Read README.md opening paragraphs for the positioning statement (structured intent-based batch development with inline critic loops)
Read .aishore/README.md 'The Intent-Driven Model' section for implicit problem statements (why intent-driven is needed vs ad-hoc)

Compose docs/PROBLEMS.md with one section per problem, each structured as: Problem heading (stated as user would search for it), Description of the pain (2-3 sentences, concrete), Evidence section (link to real discussions — GitHub issues, forum posts, articles about AI coding limitations — or mark as TODO if evidence needs gathering), How aishore addresses this (concrete explanation referencing specific aishore features)

Cover at minimum these problems: AI coding context loss between sessions, No quality gates on AI-generated code, One-shot prompting fails at project scale, No audit trail for AI development decisions, AI agents don't self-critique without structure, No way to batch AI development work
Write problem headings as search queries: e.g. 'AI-generated code has no quality gate before merge' not 'Quality Gates'
acceptanceCriteria: docs/PROBLEMS.md exists with at least 6 distinct problem sections
Each problem is stated as a user would search for it (search-friendly heading)
Each problem section includes: pain description, evidence or TODO placeholder for evidence, and how aishore addresses it
The document does not read as marketing copy — it reads as honest problem documentation with concrete solutions
Problems reference specific aishore features (maturity protocol, commander's intent, backlog system) not vague claims
